chapter_title.en: Chapter 56 – The supplication for the illnesses and diseases

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/434)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/434)
text: مَرِضَ عَلِيٌّ ( صلوات الله عليه )  فَأَتَاهُ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فَقَالَ لَهُ قُلِ اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ تَعْجِيلَ عَافِيَتِكَ وَ صَبْراً عَلَى بَلِيَّتِكَ وَ خُرُوجاً إِلَى رَحْمَتِكَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from one of his companions, from Abu Hamza,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Ali<sup>asws</sup> fell sick, so Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> came over to him<sup>asws</sup> and said to him<sup>asws</sup>: ‘Say: ‘O Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! I<sup>asws</sup> ask You<sup>azwj</sup> of quick recovery and patience upon Your<sup>azwj</sup> affliction, and an exit into Your<sup>azwj</sup> Mercy’’.
en.sarwar: 16. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from certain individuals of his people from abu Hamza from abu Ja’far (a.s.) who has said the following: “Once, Amir al-Mu’minin, Ali ibn abu Talib (a.s.) became ill. The Messenger of Allah visited him and said, ‘Say, O Lord, I plead before You for a quick recovery, (the ability to have) patience with misfortune from You and coming out of it into Your favors and mercy.”
